paper_title: Cryptotephra Analysis and Data from Cascade Lake, Alaska

methods.description: Process for analyzing cryptotephra in sediment cores to validate and improve age-depth models using glass shard concentration profiles and geochemical analysis.

steps.description: Targeted cryptotephra analyses were undertaken using contiguous 1-cm-thick subsamples from 1.42 m composite depth to the surface.
steps.searchable_summary: collect 1-cm-thick subsamples from sediment cores for cryptotephra analysis

steps.description: Standard methods were used to produce glass shard concentration profiles throughout the two core sections and the heavy liquid, Lithium Heteropolytungstate (LST), was used for density separations.
steps.searchable_summary: produce glass shard concentration profiles using LST for density separation

steps.description: Glass shards for geochemical analysis were re-extracted from peaks in shard concentration using heavy liquid separation and samples were mounted in an epoxy puck and polished to expose glass surfaces before being carbon coated prior to electron probe microanalysis (EPMA).
steps.searchable_summary: re-extract and prepare glass shards for EPMA

steps.description: New data are reported here from glass shards analysed on a JEOL 8900 Superprobe at the University of Alberta by wavelength dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (WDS) following established protocols.
steps.searchable_summary: perform WDS analysis on glass shards using JEOL 8900 Superprobe

steps.description: A standard suite of ten elements was measured using a 5 μm beam with 15 keV accelerating voltage and 6 nA beam current.
steps.searchable_summary: measure ten elements using EPMA with specified beam settings

steps.description: Na, and if necessary, Si, were corrected for Time Dependent Intensity (TDI) loss (or gain) using a self-calibrated correction with Probe for EPMA software.
steps.searchable_summary: apply TDI correction to Na and Si using Probe for EPMA software

steps.description: Two secondary standards of known composition were run concurrently with all tephra samples: ID 3506, a Lipari rhyolite obsidian, and a reference sample of Old Crow tephra.
All results were normalised to 100% and are presented as weight percent (wt%) oxides.
steps.searchable_summary: normalize results to 100% and present as wt% oxides

methods.description: Analysis of cryptotephra data to identify geochemical populations and correlate with known eruptions.

steps.description: Twenty-eight peaks were chosen for geochemical analysis based on the relative abundances of shards counted at those depths.
steps.searchable_summary: select peaks for geochemical analysis based on shard abundance

steps.description: Geochemical analyses were performed on single grains, but 15 of the peaks chosen resulted in fewer than five shards exposed on the EPMA puck surface.
steps.searchable_summary: perform geochemical analysis on single grains

steps.description: Of the remaining 13 samples, five have dominant unique geochemical populations, six have multiple identifiable trends/populations, and two have sparse shards with no discernible geochemical trends.
steps.searchable_summary: identify geochemical populations and trends in samples

steps.description: Table 3 and Fig. 3 show the samples analysed, the average major element EPMA data for identified geochemical populations and any geochemical correlations to known eruptions with associated chronological data or similarities to known volcanic sources.
steps.searchable_summary: correlate geochemical data with known eruptions
